g.text: • Central finite difference: perturb ±ε, evaluate forward model
SVD(Jw) = UΣVT → σmin = smallest singular value ("weakest direction")
• Jw is noise-normalized → σmin is directly interpretable as SNR
• σmin<1: unobservable | σmin>1: detectable | σmin≈1: threshold
○ Linear: allows "compensation" — hides failures in easy cases
○ Min: wastes effort on physically impossible "dead zones"
• Sigmoid focuses gradient on transition zone where improvement matters
• Identify worst-case load via moment estimator: M = F1·s1 + F2·s2
• Run forward model → internal wrenches → Von Mises stress per link
• Factor of Safety: FOS = σyield / σvonMises (must be ≥ 1 for all links)

text.tspan: • Transverse Forces: Applied perpendicular to the local longitudinal axis
• Concentric Forces: Pass through the geometric center (no offset)
• Follower Forces: Defined relative to the local frame, not the global frame
• Elastic Deformation: Purely elastic (non-plastic) with full recovery
• Static Equilibrium: Strictly time-invariant; no kinetic motion or vibration
• Computes the proximal wrench W = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz] required to hold quasi-static
equilibrium configuration under the applied load and tendon tensions

g.text: Return: W = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz]
